title: Add per-skill hash pinning to soul_manager to separate content-audit and integrity-at-load trust boundaries
description: The current skill security audit in soul_manager.audit_skill() inspects skill code at import time — this is a single-plane defense that addresses the 'what does the code say' trust boundary. But it does not address the 'is this the same file that was audited' trust boundary. A skill file that passes content inspection can later be replaced on disk (e.g., by a compromised feed fetcher, a malicious explore pipeline output, or a lateral agent write) without triggering re-audit. Add a two-phase trust model: (1) on first audit-pass, record SHA-256 of the skill file into a locked manifest (soul/skill_manifest.json); (2) on every subsequent load/execution, verify the file hash matches the manifest before running. If hash diverges, block execution and flag as tampered — do not re-run audit silently. These two checks operate on independent trust planes: content-audit catches malicious intent in new skills, hash-pinning catches post-audit substitution at the filesystem layer.
rationale: The reading note's core insight: 'every trust boundary on the dependency chain is an independent attack surface, and upstream defense is completely ineffective against downstream attack.' The current audit checks code content (one plane). File-system substitution after audit is a different plane — SHA pinning of the audited artifact defends it. The note explicitly says the gap between 'is this that commit' and 'was the git host tampered' is not a vulnerability but an architectural trust misalignment. Same structure here: content-audit and load-time integrity are not the same check and cannot substitute for each other.
diff_description: In soul_manager.py: (1) After audit_skill() returns PASS, compute hashlib.sha256 of the skill file bytes and write {skill_name: {path: ..., sha256: ..., audited_at: ...}} into soul/skill_manifest.json. (2) Add a load_skill(name) guard that reads the manifest, recomputes the file hash, and raises SkillTamperError (block + log) if they diverge. (3) If skill_manifest.json entry is missing for a skill (first run on existing skills), treat as UNAUDITED and route through full audit before allowing execution. skill_manifest.json should be append-only from the agent's perspective — only audit_skill() may write to it, never the skill loader.
source_note: 2026-03-28_1-信任边界是可以被逐层击穿的而且每一层都是独立的.md
